</p><div><div><br /></div><div><b><font color='#8b0000'>Important Note:</font></b> T<span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>his item requires </span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>an ArcGIS Online organizational subscription or an ArcGIS Developer account but </span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>does not consume credits</span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>. </span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>To access this item, you'll need to</span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'> do one of the following:</span></div><div><ul><li><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>Sign in with an account that is a member of an organizational subscription</span></li><li><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'><span style='line-height: 17.9998016357422px;'>Sign in with </span>a developer account</span></li><li><a href='http://goto.arcgisonline.com/developers/register' style='line-height: 1.3846;' target='_blank'>Register an application</a><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'> and use your application's credentials</span></li></ul><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>If you don't have an account, you can sign up for a </span><a href='http://goto.arcgisonline.com/features/trial' style='line-height: 1.3846;' target='_blank'>free trial of ArcGIS</a><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'> </span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>or a</span><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'> </span><a href='http://goto.arcgisonline.com/developers/signup' style='line-height: 1.3846;' target='_blank'>free ArcGIS Developer account</a><span style='line-height: 1.3846;'>.</span></div></div></div>

There are no additional usage costs.</span></p><p style='margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; padding: 0px; font-size: 12px; line-height: normal; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><span style='font-size: 10pt; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif;'>Service is based on Landsat 8 OLI imagery downloaded from USGS. The services are hosted by Esri for ArcGIS Online users to use as dynamic image services in WebMaps and  ArcGIS Desktop, Web and Mobile applications.</span><span style='font-size: 12pt; font-family: '></span></p><p style='margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; padding: 0px; font-size: 12px; line-height: normal; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><span style='font-size: 10pt; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif;'>Exporting of imagery is limited to max of 4000x4000 cols,rows per request and permitted for limited requests by each organization.  No systematic scraping or making of large numbers of export request to obtain local copies of the imagery is allowed. </span></p><p style='margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; padding: 0px; font-size: 12px; line-height: normal; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><span style='font-size: 10pt; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif;'>Should users require the full scenes, please look to Landatlook.usgs.gov</span><span style='font-family: Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;'> to review and download full scenes.</span></p>

If you don't have an organizational account, you can sign up for a <a href='https://www.arcgis.com/about/trial.html' style='color: rgb(33, 117, 155); outline: none !important;' target='_blank'>30 day trial of ArcGIS Online</a>.</div><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><br /></div><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><b><font color='#b22222'>This layer is in Beta:</font></b> Participating in the Landscape Analysis beta allows you to learn about this new layer, test your workflows using this content and prepare for upcoming upgrades.  Additionally, the feedback you provide through your participation and use inform Esri's software development teams of required and suggested changes that strongly influence the final, released layer.  Your feedback is important to us! Please let us know about your experience at the <a href='http://forums.arcgis.com/forums/232-Landscape-Maps-and-Data' style='color: rgb(33, 117, 155); outline: none !important;' target='_blank'>ArcGIS Online forum</a>   </div><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><br /></div><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'>During the beta period, there is no cost for using this layer.  There will be announcements in advance of any transition from free use to credit-based use, if applicable.  This layer may change extensively for the final release.</div>

Item: 6dea85d10b41417aa13a52986bb359c8
Title: Local Impact & Owner Occupied Housing
Owner: FedGISContent
Type: Web Map
Matched: esrilogo
Replacements: 1
Open item
Old source
<span style='overflow: auto;'><img src='http://downloads.esri.com/blogs/arcgisonline/esrilogo_new.png' /> </span><div><span style='overflow: auto;'>This work is licensed under the Web Services and API Terms of Use.   <br /><a href='http://links.esri.com/e800-summary' target='_blank'><b>View Summary</b></a>  |  <a href='http://links.esri.com/agol_tou' target='_blank'><b>View Terms of Use</b></a> </span><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for demonstrational and informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'><p><b>SURGE IMPACT ASSESSMENTS ARE BASED ON WORST CASE SCENARIOS USING MAXIMUM OF MAXIMUM (MOM) HURRICANE STORM SURGE PER SAFFIR-SIMPSON HURRICANE CATEGORY, WHICH MAY RESULT IN CONSERVATIVE ESTIMATES OF IMPACTS. Please note that no single hurricane will produce the regional flooding depicted in the MOMs or the loss estimations, risk and vulnerability assessments and potential impacts. Instead, the product is intended to estimate the worst case high water values for particular locations for hurricane evacuation planning. <span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'> </span>Official forecasts and products are provided by the NOAA National Hurricane Center for specific storms based on official watches or warnings, loss estimation and potential impact assessments may be refined and updated as necessary.</b></p><p><b>Loss estimates or totals only reflect data for affected census tracts/blocks included in the study regions.<span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'>  </span>The estimates of social and economic impacts contained in these reports were produced using Hazus loss estimation methodology software which is based on current scientific and engineering knowledge. There are uncertainties inherent in any loss estimation, hurricane modeling (including storm surge modeling), ground elevation data, and methodologies and techniques. Therefore, there may be significant differences between the modeled results and the actual social and economic losses following a specific storm event. These results can be improved by using enhanced inventory, geotechnical, or observed meteorological data. </b></p><b><span style='line-height: 115%; font-family: &quot;calibri&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 11pt; mso-fareast-font-family: calibri; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot;; mso-ascii-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-ansi-language: en-us; mso-fareast-language: en-us; mso-bidi-language: ar-sa;'>User assumes all risk related to the use of this data.<span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'>  </span>FEMA provides this data &quot;as is&quot; and disclaims any and all warranties, whether express or implied, including (without limitation) any implied warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose, and there are no express or implied guarantees of accuracy of the data.<span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'>  </span>In no event will FEMA or any other Federal Agency be liable to you or to any third party for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or exemplary damages or lost profit resulting from any use or misuse of this data.</span></b><span style='display: none;'></span></span></div></div>

</p><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for demonstrational and informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'><p><b>SURGE IMPACT ASSESSMENTS ARE BASED ON WORST CASE SCENARIOS USING MAXIMUM OF MAXIMUM (MOM) HURRICANE STORM SURGE PER SAFFIR-SIMPSON HURRICANE CATEGORY, WHICH MAY RESULT IN CONSERVATIVE ESTIMATES OF IMPACTS. Please note that no single hurricane will produce the regional flooding depicted in the MOMs or the loss estimations, risk and vulnerability assessments and potential impacts. Instead, the product is intended to estimate the worst case high water values for particular locations for hurricane evacuation planning. <span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'> </span>Official forecasts and products are provided by the NOAA National Hurricane Center for specific storms based on official watches or warnings, loss estimation and potential impact assessments may be refined and updated as necessary.</b></p><p><b>Loss estimates or totals only reflect data for affected census tracts/blocks included in the study regions.<span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'>  </span>The estimates of social and economic impacts contained in these reports were produced using Hazus loss estimation methodology software which is based on current scientific and engineering knowledge. There are uncertainties inherent in any loss estimation, hurricane modeling (including storm surge modeling), ground elevation data, and methodologies and techniques. Therefore, there may be significant differences between the modeled results and the actual social and economic losses following a specific storm event. These results can be improved by using enhanced inventory, geotechnical, or observed meteorological data. </b></p><b><span style='line-height: 115%; font-family: &quot;calibri&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 11pt; mso-fareast-font-family: calibri; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot;; mso-ascii-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-ansi-language: en-us; mso-fareast-language: en-us; mso-bidi-language: ar-sa;'>User assumes all risk related to the use of this data.<span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'>  </span>FEMA provides this data &quot;as is&quot; and disclaims any and all warranties, whether express or implied, including (without limitation) any implied warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose, and there are no express or implied guarantees of accuracy of the data.<span style='mso-spacerun: yes;'>  </span>In no event will FEMA or any other Federal Agency be liable to you or to any third party for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or exemplary damages or lost profit resulting from any use or misuse of this data.</span></b><span style='display: none;'></span></span></div></div>

Item: c971d4979d0a4a90b5fdbfb265a43183
Title: #YolandaPH: Map Tour by StandbyTaskForce and GISCorps-Copy
Owner: glazou2
Type: Web Map
Matched: esrilogo
Replacements: 1
Open item
Old source
<span style='overflow: auto;'><img src='http://downloads.esri.com/blogs/arcgisonline/esrilogo_new.png' /> </span><div><span style='overflow: auto;'>This work is licensed under the Web Services and API Terms of Use.   <br /><a href='http://links.esri.com/e800-summary' target='_blank'><b>View Summary</b></a>  |  <a href='http://links.esri.com/agol_tou' target='_blank'><b>View Terms of Use</b></a> </span><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><font face='verdana, sans-serif'><span style='font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for </span><span style='font-size: 9pt;'>informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></font></div></div><div><br /></div><div><font face='Arial, sans-serif'><span style='line-height: normal;'><b>About the data:</b></span></font></div><div><font face='Arial, sans-serif'><span style='line-height: normal;'><b><br /></b></span></font></div><div><font face='Arial, sans-serif'><span style='line-height: normal;'><b>Atlantic and Central Pacific</b></span></font></div><div><ul><li><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>Forecast points:</span><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'> represent the official NHC forecast locations of the center of a tropical cyclone. Forecast center positions are given for projections valid 12, 24, 36, 48, 72, 96, and 120 h after the forecast's nominal initial time. Data source: </span><span style='line-height: 115%;'><a href='http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip'><font face='Arial' size='2'>http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip</font></a></span></li><li><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Forecast track:</span><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> This product aids in the visualization of an NHC official track forecast, the forecast points are connected by black line segments. The track line(s) are not a forecast product, however, and because there are an infinite number of ways to connect a set of forecast points, the lines should not be interpreted as representing a specific forecast for the location of a tropical cyclone in between official forecast points. It is also important to remember that tropical cyclone track forecasts are subject to error, and that the effects of a tropical cyclone can span many hundreds of miles from the center. </span></li><li><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>The Cone of Uncertainty:</span><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'> The cone represents the probable track of the center of a tropical cyclone, and is formed by enclosing the area swept out by a set of circles along the forecast track (at 12, 24, 36 hours, etc). The size of each circle is set so that two-thirds of historical official forecast errors over a 5-year sample fall within the circle. Based on forecasts over the previous 5 years, the entire track of a tropical cyclone can be expected to remain within the cone roughly 60-70% of the time. It is important to note that the area affected by a tropical cyclone can extend well beyond the confines of the cone enclosing the most likely track area of the center. </span><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>Data source: </span><a href='http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip'><font face='Arial' size='2'>http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip</font></a></li><li><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Watch/Warning:</span><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> Coastal areas placed under tropical cyclone watches or warnings are delimited by specific geographical locations known as &quot;breakpoints&quot; (e.g., a hurricane warning is in effect from breakpoint &quot;A&quot; to breakpoint &quot;B&quot;). This file consists of one or more lines connecting the breakpoints delimiting any current watches or warnings. </span><span style='line-height: 1.4062; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>Data source: <span style='font-family: Verdana;'><font face='Arial' size='2'><a href='http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip'>http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip</a> </font></span>For more information about the NHC Data Source see this document: </span><a href='http://w1.weather.gov/cio/policy/documents/shapefiles.pdf' style='line-height: 1.4062; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>http://w1.weather.gov/cio/policy/documents/shapefiles.pdf</a> </li><li><font face='Arial'>Weather Stations: </font><span style='line-height: normal; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>The Current Wind Conditions layer is a created from hourly METAR/TAF data provided from NOAA. </span><a href='http://www.aviationweather.gov/adds/metars/'>http://www.aviationweather.gov/adds/metars/</a></li></ul><div><b>Western Pacific and Indian Ocean</b></div><div><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'><br /></span></b></div><div><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Source: </span></b><a href='http://www.usno.navy.mil/JTWC/' style='line-height: normal;'><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>http://www.usno.navy.mil/JTWC/</span></a></div><div>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Organization: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Joint Typhoon Warning Center (JTWC) <b>POC:</b> Naval Meteorology and Oceanography Command; Email: </span><a href='mailto:SRNS_CNMOC_paoweb@navy.mil'><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>SRNS_CNMOC_paoweb@navy.mil</span></a><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>, Phone: <span style='white-space: nowrap;'>(228)-688-4394<a href='#' style='margin: 0px; border: currentColor; left: 0px; top: 0px; width: 16px; height: 16px; right: 0px; bottom: 0px; overflow: hidden; vertical-align: middle; float: none; display: inline; white-space: nowrap; position: static !important;'><img src='#image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABAAAAAQCAYAAAAf8/9hAAAACXBIWXMAAA7EAAAOxAGVKw4bAAAAIGNIUk0AAHolAACAgwAA+f8AAIDpAAB1MAAA6mAAADqYAAAXb5JfxUYAAAKLSURBVHjadJPfS5NhFMe/21xvuhXRyJAZroiSrJnbRdT7vrAf5HBaK5RABmEEwQIvkpZ/QRcWXdSFw5soKaF0F7qZeLO13mGBDpQsf5CoxVKHOt0Pctp2uvEdrzG/V+c553w/54HnPDIiQiGpPMETABoB2AAYd9MRAMMAvGmX+RcAyAoBVJ7gZQDtABworH4AHWmX+bOMZdkjCoXiUzabvcAwzPSsob5p/VTNY9GcdpnxdmYZ9wJThSCtCr1e/4XjuNPd3d1KjUZzaGbI27ysqzGQoggAsLa1A7ehArrDxfDNr0oBlQB+wmKxbJFEL968SxoamsjkHaPU9l9piUo6A0RE1DG2QCWdASrpDAzJM5kMI8XecdjVxfEl+K9dxFgsgUvvR6HyBKHyBAEATyKLeGSsENuNcqk5kUjEGm7fzcYqr0ClVODl99+YXEvl6+c1amjVe+ahiGGYaUEQKnmeh91uL43rqheixjpdmzCL11er0PcjhrTLvMfUJsyKYUSeyWQ6enp6tgCgrKxsfbP8bB8AdE1G89cOReMAgOv+Cag8QXRNRkXAsDwcDr+am5tLCYKA3t7eo2dG+1vVK/MfpRPtA+MIReMYaKj+/xm9MiICx3EmpVL5wefzFavValis1u1vvHMkdfykCQC0kSGUTo+Ajmnx1dSC7IGD+UUCEYGIwLKsyWazrSeTSSIiMpnNf7Ttz5+ec96fr7/VnE0mk+QfHMzV3WjcKH/4rEr05QGFIA6HY4llWRLPRER+v3/HYrFMFQSIkNra2tVQKJSlfcSyLO0LECFWq3XF6XRGA4HAptTsdrsXeZ6fEHtl+31nAOA4rkUulz/I5XL63dQGgHEAN8Ph8AYA/BsAt4ube4GblQIAAAAASUVORK5CYII=' style='margin: 0px; border: currentColor; left: 0px; top: 0px; width: 16px; height: 16px; right: 0px; bottom: 0px; overflow: hidden; vertical-align: middle; float: none; display: inline; white-space: nowrap; position: static !important;' /></a></span>.</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Scale/Resolution</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>: Not specified<b>                    </b></span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Update Frequency: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>The Live Feed Methodology checks the Source for updates every 15 minutes.</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>N</span></b><span style='background: white; color: black; font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>orth Pacific and North Indian Ocean tropical cyclone warnings are updated every 6 hours and South Indian and South Pacific Ocean tropical cyclone warnings are updated every 12 hours.</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Area Covered: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>South &amp; West Pacific Ocean and the Indian Ocean<b>              </b></span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Description</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>: This map service describes the path and forecast path of tropical activity. </span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Access and Use Constraints: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>No restrictions</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Forecast points</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> represent the official JTWC forecast locations of the center of a tropical cyclone. Forecast center positions are given for projections valid 12, 24, 36, 48, and 72 h after the forecast's nominal initial time. </span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Forecast track</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> This product aids in the visualization of an official track forecast, the forecast points are connected by black line segments. The track line(s) are not a forecast product, however, and because there are an infinite number of ways to connect a set of forecast points, the lines should not be interpreted as representing a specific forecast for the location of a tropical cyclone in between official forecast points. It is also important to remember that tropical cyclone track forecasts are subject to error, and that the effects of a tropical cyclone can span many hundreds of miles from the center. </span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>The Cone of Uncertainty</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> T<span style='background: white; color: black;'>he shaded area around the forecast track is called the &quot;area of uncertainty.&quot; The area of uncertainty is calculated by adding the JTWC 5-year running mean forecast track error to the forecast 34 knot wind radii at each forecast time. Since JTWC does not forecast wind radii for the 96- and 120-hour forecast times, the area of uncertainty is calculated by adding the 72-hour 34 knot radii to the forecast track error at those times. Thus, the shading highlights the area that may be effected by wind speeds exceeding 34 knots for a given JTWC forecast, based on historical track forecast errors. However, this calculation does NOT account for uncertainty in the track forecast based on the spread of the numerical model spread or other forecast guidance at a particular forecast time.</span> </span></p></div><p></p>        </div>
New source
<p>
    <img src="https://www.esri.com/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/common/logos/esri-logo.jpg" alt="Esri logo" width="113" height="39">
</p>
<p style="color:rgb(74,74,74);font-family:'Avenir Next',Avenir,'Helvetica Neue',sans-serif;font-size:16px;margin:0 0 1rem;">
    This work is licensed under the Esri Master License Agreement.
</p>
<p style="color:rgb(74,74,74);font-family:'Avenir Next',Avenir,'Helvetica Neue',sans-serif;font-size:16px;margin:0;">
    <a style="color:rgb(0,97,155);text-decoration:none;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://goto.arcgis.com/termsofuse/viewsummary"><strong>View Summary</strong></a> | <a style="color:rgb(0,97,155);text-decoration:none;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://goto.arcgis.com/termsofuse/viewtermsofuse"><strong>View Terms of Use</strong></a>
</p><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: &quot;verdana&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><font face='verdana, sans-serif'><span style='font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for </span><span style='font-size: 9pt;'>informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></font></div></div><div><br /></div><div><font face='Arial, sans-serif'><span style='line-height: normal;'><b>About the data:</b></span></font></div><div><font face='Arial, sans-serif'><span style='line-height: normal;'><b><br /></b></span></font></div><div><font face='Arial, sans-serif'><span style='line-height: normal;'><b>Atlantic and Central Pacific</b></span></font></div><div><ul><li><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>Forecast points:</span><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'> represent the official NHC forecast locations of the center of a tropical cyclone. Forecast center positions are given for projections valid 12, 24, 36, 48, 72, 96, and 120 h after the forecast's nominal initial time. Data source: </span><span style='line-height: 115%;'><a href='http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip'><font face='Arial' size='2'>http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip</font></a></span></li><li><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Forecast track:</span><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> This product aids in the visualization of an NHC official track forecast, the forecast points are connected by black line segments. The track line(s) are not a forecast product, however, and because there are an infinite number of ways to connect a set of forecast points, the lines should not be interpreted as representing a specific forecast for the location of a tropical cyclone in between official forecast points. It is also important to remember that tropical cyclone track forecasts are subject to error, and that the effects of a tropical cyclone can span many hundreds of miles from the center. </span></li><li><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>The Cone of Uncertainty:</span><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'> The cone represents the probable track of the center of a tropical cyclone, and is formed by enclosing the area swept out by a set of circles along the forecast track (at 12, 24, 36 hours, etc). The size of each circle is set so that two-thirds of historical official forecast errors over a 5-year sample fall within the circle. Based on forecasts over the previous 5 years, the entire track of a tropical cyclone can be expected to remain within the cone roughly 60-70% of the time. It is important to note that the area affected by a tropical cyclone can extend well beyond the confines of the cone enclosing the most likely track area of the center. </span><span style='font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>Data source: </span><a href='http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip'><font face='Arial' size='2'>http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip</font></a></li><li><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Watch/Warning:</span><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> Coastal areas placed under tropical cyclone watches or warnings are delimited by specific geographical locations known as &quot;breakpoints&quot; (e.g., a hurricane warning is in effect from breakpoint &quot;A&quot; to breakpoint &quot;B&quot;). This file consists of one or more lines connecting the breakpoints delimiting any current watches or warnings. </span><span style='line-height: 1.4062; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>Data source: <span style='font-family: Verdana;'><font face='Arial' size='2'><a href='http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip'>http://www.nhc.noaa.gov/gis/forecast/archive/ep042013_5day_latest.zip</a> </font></span>For more information about the NHC Data Source see this document: </span><a href='http://w1.weather.gov/cio/policy/documents/shapefiles.pdf' style='line-height: 1.4062; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>http://w1.weather.gov/cio/policy/documents/shapefiles.pdf</a> </li><li><font face='Arial'>Weather Stations: </font><span style='line-height: normal; font-family: Arial, sans-serif;'>The Current Wind Conditions layer is a created from hourly METAR/TAF data provided from NOAA. </span><a href='http://www.aviationweather.gov/adds/metars/'>http://www.aviationweather.gov/adds/metars/</a></li></ul><div><b>Western Pacific and Indian Ocean</b></div><div><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'><br /></span></b></div><div><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Source: </span></b><a href='http://www.usno.navy.mil/JTWC/' style='line-height: normal;'><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>http://www.usno.navy.mil/JTWC/</span></a></div><div>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Organization: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Joint Typhoon Warning Center (JTWC) <b>POC:</b> Naval Meteorology and Oceanography Command; Email: </span><a href='mailto:SRNS_CNMOC_paoweb@navy.mil'><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>SRNS_CNMOC_paoweb@navy.mil</span></a><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>, Phone: <span style='white-space: nowrap;'>(228)-688-4394<a href='#' style='margin: 0px; border: currentColor; left: 0px; top: 0px; width: 16px; height: 16px; right: 0px; bottom: 0px; overflow: hidden; vertical-align: middle; float: none; display: inline; white-space: nowrap; position: static !important;'><img src='#image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABAAAAAQCAYAAAAf8/9hAAAACXBIWXMAAA7EAAAOxAGVKw4bAAAAIGNIUk0AAHolAACAgwAA+f8AAIDpAAB1MAAA6mAAADqYAAAXb5JfxUYAAAKLSURBVHjadJPfS5NhFMe/21xvuhXRyJAZroiSrJnbRdT7vrAf5HBaK5RABmEEwQIvkpZ/QRcWXdSFw5soKaF0F7qZeLO13mGBDpQsf5CoxVKHOt0Pctp2uvEdrzG/V+c553w/54HnPDIiQiGpPMETABoB2AAYd9MRAMMAvGmX+RcAyAoBVJ7gZQDtABworH4AHWmX+bOMZdkjCoXiUzabvcAwzPSsob5p/VTNY9GcdpnxdmYZ9wJThSCtCr1e/4XjuNPd3d1KjUZzaGbI27ysqzGQoggAsLa1A7ehArrDxfDNr0oBlQB+wmKxbJFEL968SxoamsjkHaPU9l9piUo6A0RE1DG2QCWdASrpDAzJM5kMI8XecdjVxfEl+K9dxFgsgUvvR6HyBKHyBAEATyKLeGSsENuNcqk5kUjEGm7fzcYqr0ClVODl99+YXEvl6+c1amjVe+ahiGGYaUEQKnmeh91uL43rqheixjpdmzCL11er0PcjhrTLvMfUJsyKYUSeyWQ6enp6tgCgrKxsfbP8bB8AdE1G89cOReMAgOv+Cag8QXRNRkXAsDwcDr+am5tLCYKA3t7eo2dG+1vVK/MfpRPtA+MIReMYaKj+/xm9MiICx3EmpVL5wefzFavValis1u1vvHMkdfykCQC0kSGUTo+Ajmnx1dSC7IGD+UUCEYGIwLKsyWazrSeTSSIiMpnNf7Ttz5+ec96fr7/VnE0mk+QfHMzV3WjcKH/4rEr05QGFIA6HY4llWRLPRER+v3/HYrFMFQSIkNra2tVQKJSlfcSyLO0LECFWq3XF6XRGA4HAptTsdrsXeZ6fEHtl+31nAOA4rkUulz/I5XL63dQGgHEAN8Ph8AYA/BsAt4ube4GblQIAAAAASUVORK5CYII=' style='margin: 0px; border: currentColor; left: 0px; top: 0px; width: 16px; height: 16px; right: 0px; bottom: 0px; overflow: hidden; vertical-align: middle; float: none; display: inline; white-space: nowrap; position: static !important;' /></a></span>.</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Scale/Resolution</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>: Not specified<b>                    </b></span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Update Frequency: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>The Live Feed Methodology checks the Source for updates every 15 minutes.</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>N</span></b><span style='background: white; color: black; font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>orth Pacific and North Indian Ocean tropical cyclone warnings are updated every 6 hours and South Indian and South Pacific Ocean tropical cyclone warnings are updated every 12 hours.</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Area Covered: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>South &amp; West Pacific Ocean and the Indian Ocean<b>              </b></span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Description</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>: This map service describes the path and forecast path of tropical activity. </span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Access and Use Constraints: </span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>No restrictions</span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Forecast points</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> represent the official JTWC forecast locations of the center of a tropical cyclone. Forecast center positions are given for projections valid 12, 24, 36, 48, and 72 h after the forecast's nominal initial time. </span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>Forecast track</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> This product aids in the visualization of an official track forecast, the forecast points are connected by black line segments. The track line(s) are not a forecast product, however, and because there are an infinite number of ways to connect a set of forecast points, the lines should not be interpreted as representing a specific forecast for the location of a tropical cyclone in between official forecast points. It is also important to remember that tropical cyclone track forecasts are subject to error, and that the effects of a tropical cyclone can span many hundreds of miles from the center. </span></p>  <p style='line-height: normal;'><b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'>The Cone of Uncertainty</span></b><span style='font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,&quot;sans-serif&quot;;'> T<span style='background: white; color: black;'>he shaded area around the forecast track is called the &quot;area of uncertainty.&quot; The area of uncertainty is calculated by adding the JTWC 5-year running mean forecast track error to the forecast 34 knot wind radii at each forecast time. Since JTWC does not forecast wind radii for the 96- and 120-hour forecast times, the area of uncertainty is calculated by adding the 72-hour 34 knot radii to the forecast track error at those times. Thus, the shading highlights the area that may be effected by wind speeds exceeding 34 knots for a given JTWC forecast, based on historical track forecast errors. However, this calculation does NOT account for uncertainty in the track forecast based on the spread of the numerical model spread or other forecast guidance at a particular forecast time.</span> </span></p></div><p></p>        </div>

Title: The Iron Path

<img src='http://downloads.esri.com/blogs/arcgisonline/esrilogo_new.png' style='max-width: 100%; height: auto; border: 0px none; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px;' /><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px;'>This work is licensed under the Web Services and API Terms of Use.   </span><br /><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px;'>                               </span><a href='http://links.esri.com/e800-summary' style='color: rgb(33, 117, 155); text-decoration-line: none; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; outline: none !important;' target='_blank'>View Summary</a><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px;'>  |  </span><a href='http://links.esri.com/agol_tou' style='color: rgb(33, 117, 155); text-decoration-line: none; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; outline: none !important;' target='_blank'>View Terms of Use</a><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px;'> </span>

Item: b31b99e09efe459c9b57a193e27f572f
Title: How to Build Rotating Globes in ArcGIS Pro
Owner: grey9005@esri.com_Story
Type: Web Mapping Application
Matched: esrilogo
Replacements: 1

Title: Moore Oklahoma Post-Tornado Imagery_2013

Open item
Old source
Data Source: Bearing Tree Land Survey <a href='http://www.btls.us/'>http://www.btls.us/</a><div><br /></div><div><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'> </span><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; overflow: auto; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><img src='http://downloads.esri.com/blogs/arcgisonline/esrilogo_new.png' style='border: 0px currentcolor;' /></span><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px; font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><span style='overflow: auto;'>This work is licensed under the Web Services and API Terms of Use. <br /><a href='http://links.esri.com/e800-summary' style='color: rgb(33, 117, 155);' target='_blank'><b>View Summary</b></a> | <a href='http://links.esri.com/agol_tou' style='color: rgb(33, 117, 155);' target='_blank'><b>View Terms of Use</b></a></span><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px;'><span style='font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px;'><span style='font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for demonstrational and informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></div></div><div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div></div></div>
New source
Data Source: Bearing Tree Land Survey <a href='http://www.btls.us/'>http://www.btls.us/</a><div><br /></div><div><span style='font-family: Verdana, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 18px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'> </span><p>
    <img src="https://www.esri.com/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/common/logos/esri-logo.jpg" alt="Esri logo" width="113" height="39">
</p>
<p style="color:rgb(74,74,74);font-family:'Avenir Next',Avenir,'Helvetica Neue',sans-serif;font-size:16px;margin:0 0 1rem;">
    This work is licensed under the Esri Master License Agreement.
</p>
<p style="color:rgb(74,74,74);font-family:'Avenir Next',Avenir,'Helvetica Neue',sans-serif;font-size:16px;margin:0;">
    <a style="color:rgb(0,97,155);text-decoration:none;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://goto.arcgis.com/termsofuse/viewsummary"><strong>View Summary</strong></a> | <a style="color:rgb(0,97,155);text-decoration:none;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://goto.arcgis.com/termsofuse/viewtermsofuse"><strong>View Terms of Use</strong></a>
</p><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px;'><span style='font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div style='margin: 0px; padding: 0px;'><span style='font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for demonstrational and informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></div></div><div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div></div></div>
Item: 65b542e42f1547aebdf8fc5f9189b9ea
Title: Monitoring a Severe Wildfire Season - 2017
Owner: StoryMaps
Type: Web Mapping Application
Matched: https://downloads.esri.com/blogs/arcgisonline/esrilogo_new.png, esrilogo
Replacements: 1
Open item
Old source
<div style='font-family: &quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size: 17px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>About the Data in the Web Map:</p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>The Wildfire Activity layer displays large active fire incidents and situation reports that have been entered into the National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC) database by local emergency response teams. <span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>The Active Fire Perimeters layer is a product of Geospatial Multi-Agency Coordination (GeoMAC). In order to give fire managers near real-time information, fire perimeter data is updated daily based upon input from incident intelligence sources, GPS data, infrared (IR) imagery from fixed wing and satellite platforms </span><a href='http://rmgsc.cr.usgs.gov/outgoing/GeoMAC/geomac_download_procedures.pdf' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_self'>GeoMAC Wildland fire perimeter data</a><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'> provided by the GeoMAC site are derived from data produced by GIS </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>specialists working on each incident. Perimeter data displayed in and delivered by the GeoMAC </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>application are not the final or official perimeters for any incident and are provided for informational </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>purposes only. The final official perimeter should be obtained from the host unit, which can be </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>determined by looking at the Unit Id for any specific fire. The host unit is responsible for producing official </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>and final perimeters for all incidents in their jurisdiction. </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>Data source: </span><a href='http://rmgsc.cr.usgs.gov/outgoing/GeoMAC/current_year_fire_data/current_year_all_states/' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>USGS Rocky Mountain Geographic Science Center Outgoing Data Sets</a>, also<span style='line-height: 1.40625;'> see GeoMAC </span><a href='http://rmgsc.cr.usgs.gov/outgoing/GeoMAC/current_year_fire_data/current_year_all_states/perimeters_dd83_METADATA.htm' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_blank'>metadata </a><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>for more information.</span></p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>The MODIS thermal layer is created from the MODIS satellite detection system and represents hot spots that could be potential fire locations in the last 24 hour period at a horizontal resolution of 1 km and temporal resolution of 1 to 2 days. For information see our explanation in the description <a href='http://tmservices1.esri.com/arcgis/rest/services/LiveFeeds/MODIS_Thermal/MapServer' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_blank'>here</a>. Data source: <a href='http://earthdata.nasa.gov/data/near-real-time-data/firms/active-fire-data' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>NASA EOSDIS website</a></p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>The Current Wind Conditions layer is created from hourly METAR/TAF data provided from NOAA. Data Source: <a href='http://www.aviationweather.gov/adds/metars/' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>NOAA Aviation Weather Center</a><br /></p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>The Wildland Fire Potential (WFP) map is a raster geospatial product produced by the USDA Forest Service, Fire Modeling Institute that is intended to be used in analyses of wildfire risk or hazardous fuels prioritization at large landscapes (100s of square miles) up through regional or national scales. The specific objective of the 2014 WFP map is to depict the relative potential for wildfire. Data source: </span><a href='http://www.firelab.org/fmi/data-products/229-wildland-fire-potential-wfp' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>Fire, Fuel, and Smoke Science Program</a><br /></p></div><span style='font-family: &quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size: 17px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255); overflow: auto;'><div><br /></div><img alt='' src='https://downloads.esri.com/blogs/arcgisonline/esrilogo_new.png' style='max-width: 100%; height: auto;' /><br /></span><div style='font-family: &quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size: 17px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><span style='overflow: auto;'>This work is licensed under the Web Services and API Terms of Use. <br /><a href='http://links.esri.com/e800-summary' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_blank'>View Summary</a> | <a href='http://links.esri.com/agol_tou' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_blank'>View Terms of Use</a></span><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for demonstration and informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></div></div>
New source
<div style='font-family: &quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size: 17px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);'><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>About the Data in the Web Map:</p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>The Wildfire Activity layer displays large active fire incidents and situation reports that have been entered into the National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC) database by local emergency response teams. <span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>The Active Fire Perimeters layer is a product of Geospatial Multi-Agency Coordination (GeoMAC). In order to give fire managers near real-time information, fire perimeter data is updated daily based upon input from incident intelligence sources, GPS data, infrared (IR) imagery from fixed wing and satellite platforms </span><a href='http://rmgsc.cr.usgs.gov/outgoing/GeoMAC/geomac_download_procedures.pdf' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_self'>GeoMAC Wildland fire perimeter data</a><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'> provided by the GeoMAC site are derived from data produced by GIS </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>specialists working on each incident. Perimeter data displayed in and delivered by the GeoMAC </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>application are not the final or official perimeters for any incident and are provided for informational </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>purposes only. The final official perimeter should be obtained from the host unit, which can be </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>determined by looking at the Unit Id for any specific fire. The host unit is responsible for producing official </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>and final perimeters for all incidents in their jurisdiction. </span><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>Data source: </span><a href='http://rmgsc.cr.usgs.gov/outgoing/GeoMAC/current_year_fire_data/current_year_all_states/' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>USGS Rocky Mountain Geographic Science Center Outgoing Data Sets</a>, also<span style='line-height: 1.40625;'> see GeoMAC </span><a href='http://rmgsc.cr.usgs.gov/outgoing/GeoMAC/current_year_fire_data/current_year_all_states/perimeters_dd83_METADATA.htm' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_blank'>metadata </a><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>for more information.</span></p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>The MODIS thermal layer is created from the MODIS satellite detection system and represents hot spots that could be potential fire locations in the last 24 hour period at a horizontal resolution of 1 km and temporal resolution of 1 to 2 days. For information see our explanation in the description <a href='http://tmservices1.esri.com/arcgis/rest/services/LiveFeeds/MODIS_Thermal/MapServer' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none;' target='_blank'>here</a>. Data source: <a href='http://earthdata.nasa.gov/data/near-real-time-data/firms/active-fire-data' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>NASA EOSDIS website</a></p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'>The Current Wind Conditions layer is created from hourly METAR/TAF data provided from NOAA. Data Source: <a href='http://www.aviationweather.gov/adds/metars/' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>NOAA Aviation Weather Center</a><br /></p><p style='margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.55rem;'><span style='line-height: 1.40625;'>The Wildland Fire Potential (WFP) map is a raster geospatial product produced by the USDA Forest Service, Fire Modeling Institute that is intended to be used in analyses of wildfire risk or hazardous fuels prioritization at large landscapes (100s of square miles) up through regional or national scales. The specific objective of the 2014 WFP map is to depict the relative potential for wildfire. Data source: </span><a href='http://www.firelab.org/fmi/data-products/229-wildland-fire-potential-wfp' style='color: rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line: none; line-height: 1.40625;' target='_self'>Fire, Fuel, and Smoke Science Program</a><br /></p></div><span style='font-family: &quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size: 17px; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255); overflow: auto;'><div><br /></div><p>
    <img src="https://www.esri.com/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/common/logos/esri-logo.jpg" alt="Esri logo" width="113" height="39">
</p>
<p style="color:rgb(74,74,74);font-family:'Avenir Next',Avenir,'Helvetica Neue',sans-serif;font-size:16px;margin:0 0 1rem;">
    This work is licensed under the Esri Master License Agreement.
</p>
<p style="color:rgb(74,74,74);font-family:'Avenir Next',Avenir,'Helvetica Neue',sans-serif;font-size:16px;margin:0;">
    <a style="color:rgb(0,97,155);text-decoration:none;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://goto.arcgis.com/termsofuse/viewsummary"><strong>View Summary</strong></a> | <a style="color:rgb(0,97,155);text-decoration:none;" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://goto.arcgis.com/termsofuse/viewtermsofuse"><strong>View Terms of Use</strong></a>
</p><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'></span></div><div><span style='color: rgb(68, 68, 68); font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt;'>This map is provided for demonstration and informational purposes and is not monitored 24/7 for accuracy and currency.</span></div></div>
